turns.content: settle something for me. speyside or islay. and dont give me a diplomatic both are great answer.
Alright, no diplomacy. I'll bite. What's your position first -- so I know whether I'm agreeing or starting a fight?
islay. no contest. if I wanted something smooth and inoffensive id drink ribena. whisky should taste like something actually happened to it.
Ha, that's a strong stance. So you're a peat and smoke person. What's your go-to Islay?
laphroaig quarter cask most of the time. its like drinking a campfire next to the sea. perfect. ardbeg uigeadail if im feeling fancy which is almost never.

Both solid choices. The Quarter Cask is excellent value for what it is. But I think there's a case for Speyside that isn't just "smooth and inoffensive" -- something like an Aberlour A'bunadh at cask strength has plenty of character.

see thats the one speyside bottle I can respect actually. abunadh is decent. but most speyside stuff is just sherry and honey and nothing else. its whisky for people who dont really like whisky. balvenie doublewood is basically a dessert.

That's harsh on the Doublewood! Though I take your point -- some Speyside malts are on the gentle side. But isn't there something to be said for subtlety and complexity? Not everything needs to punch you in the face.

I work outside in the highlands in february. I dont want subtle. I want to come home, pour something that smells like a peat bog, and feel it in my chest. subtlety is for people with office jobs and underfloor heating.

Okay, "subtlety is for people with underfloor heating" is genuinely funny. I concede that context matters -- if you're coming in from horizontal rain, a delicate Speyside probably doesn't hit the same way. What about Highland malts though? Somewhere in between?

highland park is alright. old pulteney is decent, quite like that one actually. its got a bit of salt to it cos its right on the coast in wick. but neither of them are islay. islay is just a different league for peat. the whole island smells of it when you step off the ferry.
You've been to Islay then? Distillery visits?

aye went a couple years back. did laphroaig, ardbeg, and lagavulin in one day. which was a mistake in terms of remaining upright but a great day for the palate. lagavulin 16 straight from the distillery is something else. nearly wept. well. I thought about weeping. close enough for me.
Ha, "thought about weeping" -- high praise from you, I suspect. Three distilleries in one day is ambitious. Lagavulin 16 is a classic for a reason. Did you pick up any bottles while you were there?
aye got a distillery exclusive laphroaig that was finished in some kind of weird cask. cant remember what. it was good though. gone now obviously. didnt last the month.
A month is pretty restrained for a bottle you really liked. So the verdict stands -- Islay, no compromises?
aye. peat smoke or go home. speyside can be the backup for when the good stuff runs out. which it never does because I keep buying laphroaig. system works. right ta.
A man who knows what he likes. Can't argue with a system that works. Enjoy the next dram.
